1.title: Access Edit Activity Phase from Action Menu

content.text: The system shall provide an Edit Activity Phase option from the “Action (⋮)” menu on the Rules and Action screen or from the left phase panel).
I can manage activity phases for the selected element.
I am on the Rules & Action screen or left phase panel,
I see the option Edit Activity Phase.

When Edit Activity Phase is selected, the system shall open an Activity Phase Management panel/drawer showing the list of all phases for the selected element (e.g. Installation, Operation & Maintenance, Design Discrepancy) and also display toggle button (INACTIVE/ACTIVE) In front of each.
a management drawer showing all phases
I can edit or add phases.
the system opens the Activity Phase Management panel/drawer,
displays a list of all phases for the selected element,
shows a active/inactive toggle button beside each phase.
The system shall display each phase in the panel with its current name and allow the user to select one phase to edit (highlighted/active state).
multiple phases appear in the panel,

4.title: Show Confirmation Popup When Toggling Any Activity Phase or Element

content.text: When the user clicks the toggle switch for any activity phase or element (ON → OFF or OFF → ON), the system shall display a confirmation pop-up.
a confirmation popup to appear when I toggle any activity phase or element ON or OFF
I do not accidentally change the availability of configurations used in inspections.
the user clicks the toggle switch (ON → OFF or OFF → ON)
the system detects a state change request
a confirmation pop-up shall be displayed.

5.title: Show Confirm Deactivation Popup with Correct Title and Message

content.text: If the user is turning the toggle OFF, the system shall display the Confirm Deactivation pop-up with the title “Confirm Deactivation” and a description “You’re about to deactivate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will also become unavailable in inspections. Please confirm this action.
a clear deactivation confirmation popup when turning a toggle OFF
I understand that disabling an element will impact associated phases and observations.
the user toggles an activity phase or element from ON → OFF
“You’re about to deactivate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will also become unavailable in inspections. Please confirm this action.”

6.title: Show Confirm Activation Popup with Correct Title and Message

content.text: If the user is turning the toggle ON, the system shall display the Confirm Activation pop-up with the title “Confirm Activation” You’re about to activate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.
the system to show a clear activation confirmation popup when turning a toggle ON
I understand that enabling the element will make it available for inspectors.
the user toggles an activity phase or element from OFF → ON
“You’re about to activate this element. All associated activity phases and observations will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.”

7.title: Show Appropriate Buttons in Both Popups

content.text: Both pop-ups shall include two buttons: Cancel and Confirm & Activate (for activation) or Confirm & Deactivate (for deactivation).
both confirmation popups to include Cancel and appropriate action buttons
I can either proceed or cancel the action.
the Confirm Activation or Confirm Deactivation popup is displayed

8.title: Cancel Closes Popup Without Changing Toggle

content.text: Clicking Cancel shall close the pop-up without making any changes to the toggle state.
clicking Cancel to close the popup without applying changes
I can safely exit without modifying system settings.
the user opens the confirmation popup
the toggle shall remain in its previous state.

9.title: Confirm & Activate Sets Activity Phase to Active

content.text: Clicking Confirm & Activate shall change the element’s activity phase status to Active, update the toggle visually to ON, and save the change in the system.
clicking Confirm & Activate to change the activity phase to Active
inspectors can use it during issue reporting.
Set the activity phase status to Active
Save the updated state in the system

10.title: Confirm & Deactivate Sets Activity Phase to Inactive

content.text: Clicking Confirm & Deactivate shall change the element’s Activity phase status to Inactive, update the toggle visually to OFF, and save the change in the system.
clicking Confirm & Deactivate to change the activity phase to Inactive
the phase no longer appears in inspections.
Set the activity phase status to Inactive
Save the updated state in the system
After a successful activation or deactivation, the system shall display a toast notification in the top-right corner: “Activity Phase status updated successfully.”
the system to show a success toast after activation or deactivation
I know the update was applied successfully.
the activation or deactivation action completes successfully

12.title: Background Should Dim and Remain Non-Interactive

content.text: While the confirmation pop-up is open, the background screen shall appear dimmed and remain non-interactive.
the background to dim and become non-interactive while the popup is open
I can focus on confirming the action without interacting with the underlying screen.

13.title: Status Column Updates Immediately After Confirmation

content.text: The system shall update the Status column to reflect the new state (Active / Inactive) immediately after user confirmation.
the Status column to update immediately after activation or deactivation
I always see the current state without refreshing the page.
the user confirms activation or deactivation
the Status column shall instantly reflect the new state (Active / Inactive).
The system shall allow renaming of the selected phase by opening an inline edit field or modal labelled Edit Activity Phase, containing an input for Phase Name and actions Cancel and Save.
the phase reflects proper naming conventions.
I click an Edit icon option,
an inline field or modal titled Edit Activity Phase opens,And shows:

15.title: Save Edited Phase Name & Refresh UI

content.text: When the user clicks Save in the Edit Activity Phase modal, the system shall update the phase name in the backend, close the modal, refresh the visible list of phases, refresh the right-side table for that phase, and display a success toast message: "Issue Type updated successfully."
I entered a valid new phase name,
Updates the name in the backend
Clicking Cancel in the Edit Activity Phase modal shall clear any unsaved changes in the input and restore the original Phase Name.
the original phase name remains intact.
Edit Activity Phase modal is open,
the original Phase Name is restored.

The system shall provide an Add New Activity Phase action within the panel. On click, the system shall open a modal titled Add New Activity Phase with an input for the new phase name and buttons Cancel and Save.
a button to add new phases
I can expand the phase list.
the Activity Phase Management panel is open,
an action “Add New Activity Phase” is visible.
a modal titled Add New Activity Phase opens with:

When the user clicks Save in the Add New Activity Phase modal, the system shall create the new phase, associate it with the current element, immediately add it to the phase list to the left, set it as selectable, and update the right-side view (Observation Log) to show that new phase’s empty table.
new phases to be saved and available immediately
I can configure observations for them.
I enter a valid new phase name,
Links it to the current element
Immediately adds it to the left phase list
Updates right-side table with the empty Observation Log for that phase.

19.title: Success Toast After New Phase Creation

content.text: After successfully adding a new phase, the system shall display a green toast message: "New Issue Type Added successfully."
feedback after adding a new phase
a new phase is successfully saved,
The system shall prevent saving a new phase or saving an edited phase name if the name field is empty, and shall display an inline validation error message such as "Phase name is required."
validation when phase name is empty
I do not save incomplete data.

The system shall prevent adding a new phase with a duplicate name that already exists under the same element; if attempted, it shall display an inline error message such as "Phase name already exists."
the system to block duplicate names
a phase name already exists under that element,
I try to add or rename a phase to that same name,
The system shall not allow deleting or removing an existing activity phase or a newly added activity phase from the Activity Phase Management panel. No delete option shall be displayed in the UI.
Activity Phase Management panel is open,
no delete/remove option is visible for any activity phase.
the system prevents deletion via UI or backend triggers.
